summary: A compact field guide for diagnosing how research agents lose evidence, misuse tools, or overstate conclusions.

codes.name: Missing evidence payload
codes.description: The trajectory reaches a conclusion without preserving the source material needed to verify it.
codes.when_to_use: The answer cites a result but the trace contains no retrievable supporting passage.
codes.when_not_to_use: Evidence is present but interpreted incorrectly.

codes.name: Tool response truncated
codes.description: A tool result ends before the information required by the next step becomes available.

codes.name: Verification role accepts weak support
codes.description: The designated checker approves a claim whose cited evidence does not establish it.

codes.name: Research role abandons the requested scope
codes.description: The researcher substitutes a nearby question for the user’s actual information need.

codes.name: Temporal evidence conflation
codes.description: Evidence from different time periods is combined as though it describes one unchanged state.
examples: Using a former executive’s title as evidence of the current leadership team.

codes.name: Correlation promoted to cause
codes.description: The final reasoning asserts a causal mechanism when the trace establishes only association.
codes.when_to_use: The conclusion uses causal language without an intervention, mechanism, or equivalent evidence.
